news.title: Indexes Wobble Near Flatline. Wall Street Is in Wait-and-See Mode.
news.summary: The stock market is looking for direction, dropping into the red in midday trading following a green open.  The S&P 500, Nasdaq, and Dow are wobbling at the flatline.  Equities are looking for direction in the early stages of the earnings season in what Nationwide Investment Management Group's chief market strategist Mark Hackett called "wait-and-see mode."

news.title: The Market’s Hottest Stock Is Turning Cold
news.summary: Sandisk  has been one of the hottest U.S. tech stocks over the past year but it’s starting to cool off. news.summary: The PHLX Semiconductor Index is down 4.3% with all components in the red–with one big exception.  Nvidia is up roughly 1%.  The index gained more than 87% in that timeframe, compared with Nvidia’s nearly 15% gain, according to Dow Jones Market Data.
